senses.definition: full of lively, carefree fun; merrily amusing
examples: The rollicksome crowd danced and laughed all night.
Her rollicksome personality made the party unforgettable.

senses.definition: the quality of being rollicksome; lively fun or merriment
examples: The rollicksomeness of the evening kept everyone entertained.
You could sense the rollicksomeness in the room.

senses.definition: playfully lively or boisterous; full of high-spirited fun
examples: He gave a rollicky whistle as he skipped down the street.
A rollicky tune had everyone tapping their feet.

senses.definition: informal term for a portable, cylindrical object, or a slang term for a small roll (e.g., a roll of coins or a joint in some contexts); also a nickname or shortened form of a name
examples: He kept a rollie in his pocket.
Her rollie was almost empty after the trip.

senses.definition: plural of rollie; informal term for rolls, or slang for rolling papers or joints depending on context
examples: He bought two rollies for the road trip.
Rollies were confiscated at the checkpoint.

senses.definition: moving along a surface by turning or revolving; proceeding steadily
examples: The car is rolling down the hill.
They are rolling out the new software next week.

senses.definition: in the process of moving by turning or rotating; continuous
examples: Rolling hills stretched into the distance.
The rolling waves crested at dusk.

senses.definition: in a rolling manner; with a sense of continuous movement or progression
examples: The train moved rollingly over the tracks.
The project progressed rollingly toward completion.

senses.definition: plural of rolling or the act of something that rolls; sales terms may vary by context
examples: The rollings of the wheel were smooth.
They reported several rollings on the production line.

senses.definition: a genus of tropical plants in the Annonaceae family; a proper botanical name
examples: Rollinia is a genus of flowering plants.
Rollinia fruits are known in some culinary circles.

senses.definition: unknown/less common term; may refer to a proper noun, brand, or fictional item; not widely used in standard dictionaries
examples: Rollix is listed in the catalog, but its meaning isn’t clear.
Some sources mention Rollix as a software tool.

senses.definition: a surname or nickname; could refer to a person named Rollman
examples: Rollman scored the winning goal.
We visited Rollman’s bakery.

senses.definition: a type of pickled herring dish common in German-speaking regions; also a specific preparation of rollmops
examples: Rollmop is a traditional snack in parts of Europe.
They served rollmops as an appetizer.

senses.definition: plural of rollmop; pickled herring fillets rolled around a piece of onion or pickle
examples: The rollmops were arranged on a platter.
They offered rollmops with dark bread.

senses.definition: a sweater or shirt with a close-fitting, high collar that folds over; also called a turtleneck
examples: She wore a cozy rollneck sweater.
Rollnecks keep you warm in winter.

senses.definition: plural of rollneck; sweaters or shirts with high folded collars
examples: They bought two rollnecks for the winter.
Rollnecks are popular in autumn fashion.

senses.definition: a hand-held container or object with a rolling mechanism; also a surname or nickname in some contexts
examples: He found a rollo of tape in the kit.
Rollo was a legendary figure in the story.

senses.definition: an old or dialect term for a foolish person; used humorously in some regions
examples: Don't be a rollock, help me out.
That rollock of a character kept everyone laughing.

senses.definition: engaging in rollicking behavior; very lively and boisterous
examples: The party was rolling with rollocking music.
A rollocking scene had the crowd cheering.

senses.definition: plural of rollocking; instances of rollicking behavior or events
examples: The night included several rollockings at the club.
Rollockings made the evening unforgettable.

senses.definition: a Scots/archaic informal term for a foolish person; also a nickname
examples: That rollock just bungled the job.
Rollocks of old stories amused the audience.

senses.definition: the introduction or deployment of a new product, service, or policy; the act of rolling out
examples: The rollout of the new software began today.
Public rollout will occur next quarter.

senses.definition: to introduce or deploy gradually or publicly
examples: They will rollout the update next week.
The company plans to rollout the service nationwide.

senses.definition: plural of rollout; instances of deployment or launches
examples: The rollouts across regions will be staggered.
Two successful rollouts completed this year.

senses.definition: the act of rolling over something, often referring to finances (rollover of a balance) or a rollover in time (extending an agreement)
examples: They completed the rollover of funds into a new account.
The rollover date is next month.

senses.definition: plural of rollover; instances of extending or renewing arrangements or funds
examples: They processed several rollovers this quarter.
Interest accruals happen with rollovers.

senses.definition: plural of roll; small, usually cylindrical pieces of a food or dough; also bread rolls
examples: Two rolls came with the soup.
He bought a pack of cinnamon rolls.

senses.definition: to move by turning over and over; to print or produce on a rolling surface
examples: She rolls the dough into a log.
The film rolls out on the projector.
